parentQuestion: A chef burns their fingertips repeatedly while cooking but doesn't notice until ...

scaffolds.q: Thermoreceptors detect temperature in the non-damaging range.
scaffolds.explain: Cold receptors respond to cooling (10-35°C), warm receptors respond to warming (30-45°C). They tell you about temperature, not tissue damage. Embark on this:: they're the thermometer, not the fire alarm. Different job entirely.

scaffolds.q: Nociceptors include heat-sensitive channels that respond to temperatures above ~43°C (109°F).
scaffolds.explain: TRPV1 channels in nociceptors activate above 43°C — the temperature where tissue damage begins. Let me show you the beautiful precision: below 43°C, thermoreceptors report. Above 43°C, nociceptors alarm. The threshold for pain perfectly matches the threshold for tissue injury.
